content.problemStatement:
A first-year student, came to your college. Being a good senior, you must tell him if it is possible to go from College Main Gate to Hostel for him.

The college can be visualized on a 2D-plane. Suppose the College Main Gate is situated at origin i.e. at the coordinates (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) and the Hostel is situated at the coordinates (x,y)(x,y)(x,y).

As the first-year student wants to explore the college campus further, in one move, he will increase or decrease any coordinate (either the x-coordinate or the y-coordinate) by a value of **exactly** KKK.

Is it possible for the first-year student to reach the Hostel?


Input Format

First line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. Then the description of the TTT test case follows.
Each test case contains one line of input.
The first line contains three integers xxx, yyy, KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line answer containing a "YES" or "NO" (without quotes).


You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings without quotes "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers x, y and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the point (x, y) can be reached from (0,0) using moves of exactly K units, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 1 and 2 are both multiples of 1 → YES.
Test case 2: 3 is not a multiple of 2 → NO.
Test case 3: -9 and -6 are multiples of 3 → YES.
Test case 4: 12 is a multiple of 5 but -18 is not → NO.

solution.approach: Starting from (0,0) each move changes exactly one coordinate by +K or -K. After any number of moves the x‑coordinate equals a*K and the y‑coordinate equals b*K for some integers a and b. Therefore a point (x, y) is reachable iff both x and y are multiples of K, i.e., x % K == 0 and y % K == 0. The algorithm simply checks this condition for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
